The domain actions describe a "rotate" operation.
The `rotate` action takes a node `?n`, a rotation `?r`, and directions `?fromdir`, `?todir`.
It changes the heading of `?n` and starts a process involving two passes (`rotate-first-pass`, `rotate-first-pass-end`, `rotate-second-pass`, `rotate-second-pass-end`).
Actually, looking at the domain definition:
`rotate` action:
- Precondition: not rotating, NEXT-DIRECTION fromdir r todir, heading n fromdir.
- Effect: not heading n fromdir, heading n todir, rotating, node-first-pass-next n r n, increase cost.

`rotate-first-pass` action:
- Parameters: nstart, r, n1, n2, n2x, n2y, n2dir, n2setdir.
- Precondition: CONNECTED n1 n2, NEXT-DIRECTION n2dir r n2setdir, node-first-pass-next nstart r n1, at n2 n2x n2y, heading n2 n2dir.
- Effect: not node-first-pass-next nstart r n1, node-first-pass-next nstart r n2, not at n2 n2x n2y, free n2x n2y, not heading n2 n2dir, heading n2 n2setdir, increase cost.
This action moves the "cursor" from n1 to n2 in the first pass, updating the heading of n2 and freeing its old position. It seems to propagate the rotation effect down the string.

`rotate-first-pass-end` action:
- Parameters: nstart, r, n1, n2, n2x, n2y.
- Precondition: END-NODE n2, CONNECTED n1 n2, node-first-pass-next nstart r n1, at n2 n2x n2y.
- Effect: not at n2 n2x n2y, free n2x n2y, not node-first-pass-next nstart r n1, node-second-pass-next nstart, increase cost.
This ends the first pass. It clears the last node's position and sets up the second pass cursor.

`rotate-second-pass` action:
- Parameters: n1, n1x, n1y, n1dir, n2, n2x, n2y.
- Precondition: CONNECTED n1 n2, node-second-pass-next n1, at n1 n1x n1y, heading n1 n1dir, free n2x n2y, and coordinate logic (up/down/left/right).
- Effect: not node-second-pass-next n1, node-second-pass-next n2, not free n2x n2y, at n2 n2x n2y, increase cost.
This action moves the cursor in the second pass, setting the position of n2 based on n1's position and heading.

`rotate-second-pass-end` action:
- Parameters: n.
- Precondition: END-NODE n, node-second-pass-next n.
- Effect: not node-second-pass-next n, not rotating, increase cost.
This ends the rotation process.
